constructors.name: Signal
constructors.summary: Constructs an empty [Signal] with no object nor signal name bound.

constructors.name: Signal
constructors.summary: Constructs a [Signal] as a copy of the given [Signal].

constructors.name: Signal
constructors.summary: Creates a [Signal] object referencing a signal named [param signal] in the specified [param object].

members.name: Connect
members.signature: public int Connect(Callable callable, int flags)
members.summary: Connects this signal to the specified [param callable]. Optional [param flags] can be also added to configure the connection's behavior (see [enum Object.ConnectFlags] constants). You can provide additional arguments to the connected [param callable] by using [method Callable.bind]. A signal can only be connected once to the same [Callable]. If the signal is already connected, this method returns [constant ERR_INVALID_PARAMETER] and generates an error, unless the signal is connected with [constant Object.CONNECT_REFERENCE_COUNTED]. To prevent this, use [method is_connected] first to check for existing connections. [codeblock] for button in $Buttons.get_children(): button.pressed.connect(_on_pressed.bind(button)) func _on_pressed(button): print(button.name, " was pressed") [/codeblock] [b]Note:[/b] If the [param callable]'s object is freed, the connection will be lost.

members.name: Disconnect
members.summary: Disconnects this signal from the specified [Callable]. If the connection does not exist, generates an error. Use [method is_connected] to make sure that the connection exists.

members.name: Emit
members.summary: Emits this signal. All [Callable]s connected to this signal will be triggered. This method supports a variable number of arguments, so parameters can be passed as a comma separated list.

members.name: GetConnections
members.summary: Returns an [Array] of connections for this signal. Each connection is represented as a [Dictionary] that contains three entries: - [code]signal[/code] is a reference to this signal; - [code]callable[/code] is a reference to the connected [Callable]; - [code]flags[/code] is a combination of [enum Object.ConnectFlags].

members.name: GetName
members.summary: Returns the name of this signal.

members.name: GetObject
members.summary: Returns the object emitting this signal.

members.name: GetObjectId
members.summary: Returns the ID of the object emitting this signal (see [method Object.get_instance_id]).

members.name: HasConnections
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if any [Callable] is connected to this signal.

members.name: IsConnected
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the specified [Callable] is connected to this signal.

members.name: IsNull
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if this [Signal] has no object and the signal name is empty. Equivalent to [code]signal == Signal()[/code].

operators.name: operator !=
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the signals do not share the same object and name.

operators.name: operator ==
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if both signals share the same object and name.
